target.name: The Bot Company
target.founder: Kyle Vogt, Paril Jain, Luke Holoubek

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
The Bot Company is a Series B robotics and manufacturing firm founded by Kyle Vogt, Paril Jain, and Luke Holoubek, having raised $300M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine private companies and one public peer in the robotics and manufacturing sector, all at Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology, using total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $570M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables—including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M)—yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $484M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $822M. The wide dispersion in peer valuations, particularly the $10.0B outlier, introduces material uncertainty around the upper bound. Stage-progression implies a more conservative $570M, while peer-median reflects meaningfully higher valuations for comparable-stage robotics firms. Key uncertainties include the sustainability of capital-intensity valuations in this sector and whether the target's operational metrics and market position align with the higher-valued peers or the median.
